exemplar.title: To What Extent Has Metaâs Involvement in Data Privacy Scandals Such as The Cambridge Analytica Case, Impacted Its Reputation and Financial Performances?

detailedExemplarData.title: To What Extent Has Metaâs Involvement in Data Privacy Scandals Such as The Cambridge Analytica Case, Impacted Its Reputation and Financial Performances?

subject.title: Business Management
subject.description:
**IB Business Management** is a **Group 3** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **business fundamentals, human resource management, finance and accounts, marketing, and operations management**, with a strong focus on **case study analysis, real-world business applications, and strategic decision-making**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2024**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| **Assessment** | **Format** | **SL** | **HL** | **Weighting** |
|----------------|------------|--------|--------|---------------|
| **Paper 1** | Case study-based exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 25% |
| **Paper 2** | Core themes exam | 1h 30min | 1h 30min | SL: 35%, HL: 30% |
| **Paper 3** | Social enterprise case study | N/A | 1h 15min | HL: 25% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Written commentary (SL) / Research project (HL) | Various | Various | SL: 30%, HL: 20% |

strengths: Introduction communicates the Cambridge Analytica scandal clearly with sufficient context for unfamiliar readers
Research question is clearly stated, focused on measurable impacts, and well connected to analysis sections
Methodological choices (SWOT, PESTLE, financial ratios) are explained with rationale and limitations noted
weaknesses: Exclusively secondary data and no primary research limits methodological depth
Research question wording is somewhat wordy, affecting clarity
A few contextual points (e.g., wider industry privacy concerns) are briefly noted rather than fully integrated
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Two strands (topic communication and research question) consistently meet the upper band, while methodology falls in the mid-band due to reliance on secondary sources only. An overall score of 5 reflects strong focus and clear method design, tempered by the lack of primary data and minor clarity issues.

strengths: Integration of annual reports, Statista figures and FTC documents is generally relevant and purposeful
Clear and coherent explanation of business concepts (profitability, stakeholder mapping, brand image) linked to Metaâs context
Accurate and consistent use of terminology and financial concepts throughout
weaknesses: Factual inaccuracy claiming the UN established a privacy âlawâ and misplacement of regulations in PESTLE
Some citations are indirect or repeated, and a few assertions lack clear source support
Limited depth in measuring reputation impact beyond standard ratio interpretation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Source application and subject knowledge sit solidly in the mid-band with mostly effective use of material, while terminology is in the top band. Balancing these, a score of 4 accurately reflects clear understanding marred by factual slips and citation gaps.

strengths: Research spans relevant financial data and qualitative evidence directly tied to the research question
Analysis links scandal events to ratio trends and stock movements in a clear, logical manner
Arguments are developed with acknowledgment of limitations and progression from findings to recommendations
weaknesses: Depth is constrained by a three-year window and lack of statistical benchmarking
Causal links between ratio changes and scandal impact are asserted without rigorous comparative data
Evaluation of strategies remains largely descriptive, lacking critical weighing or alternative viewpoints
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All criticalâthinking strands lie in the 4â6 band, demonstrating clear analysis and relevant research but falling short of sophisticated, fully critical evaluation. A mid-band high score of 6 rewards solid thinking while recognizing remaining gaps in depth and rigor.

strengths: All required EE sections are present and logically ordered with consistent overall layout
Word count, title page, tables, figures and citations meet IB conventions
Page numbering, appendix formatting and systematic structure enhance readability
weaknesses: Duplicate or uneven Table of Contents entries disrupt signposting
Figure captions and bibliography entries show inconsistent styling and formatting
Some headings (e.g. âDiscussion:â) and equation formatting are misaligned or incomplete
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both structure and formatting strands are in the top band, with all key elements correctly applied. Minor inconsistencies warrant only minor deductions, justifying full marks (4) in Presentation.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is fully stated, focused on measurable impacts (reputation and financial performance) and tightly connected to subsequent analysis sections (SWOT, PESTLE, ratios).  Minor wordiness makes the question slightly cumbersome, but focus is consistently maintained throughout the essay, warranting the upper band.

Arguments progress logically from tools to overall judgement and acknowledge limitations.  Evaluation of strategies (rebranding, privacy investment) is present but remains largely descriptive and lacks alternative viewpoints or weighting of evidence, so the discussion is âwell-developedâ yet only âpartiallyâ critical.

The student shows clear understanding of business conceptsâprofitability, stakeholder power/interest, brand imageâand links them to Metaâs context.  Explanations of ratios and analytical tools are coherent, but occasional factual slips (e.g. UN âlawâ) and limited depth on reputation measurement prevent the work from reaching the very top band.

Terminology such as ânet profit marginâ, âasset-turnoverâ, âstakeholder mappingâ, âdefusing strategyâ and âre-orientation strategyâ is used accurately and repeatedly.  Definitions are supplied where needed, and abbreviations (ROA, ROE, P/E) are applied correctly, showing consistent mastery with only minor stylistic errors.

Analysis links scandals to declining ratios and stock prices and uses SWOT/PESTLE to explore causes.  Calculations are correct, but some conclusions (e.g. minimal immediate impact) are asserted without rigorous benchmarking or statistical testing.  The analysis is clear and usually supported, meeting the 4-6 band but nearer its lower end.

Source material (annual reports, Statista figures, FTC documents) is generally relevant and integrated into SWOT, PESTLE and ratio calculations.  Citations accompany most data points, demonstrating appropriateness, yet some quotes are repeated or loosely referenced and a few assertions lack clear evidence, so application is âmostlyâ rather than âconsistentlyâ relevant.

Only secondary sources are used and largely drawn from mainstream news, company reports and a course textbook.  The student describes why each analytical tool is chosen and notes limitations, indicating some informed selection.  However, there is no primary research or triangulation, and currency/bias issues are acknowledged but not addressed, so the work fits the mid-band rather than the highest.

The essay collates a reasonable range of financial data (2017-19 statements, stock prices) and qualitative evidence (media coverage, regulatory fines) that relate directly to the RQ.  While breadth is good, depth is limited to three years and largely secondary, so research is âclearly relevantâ but not âconsistently excellentâ.

The essay includes word count, title, tables, figures and systematic citation formatting.  Minor inconsistencies (some line-break errors, uncertain page numbering in extract) are present but layout broadly follows EE conventions, meeting the criteria for correct application.

The student introduces the Cambridge Analytica scandal clearly, defines the organisation, states why the issue is significant, and links it explicitly to both reputation and financial performance.  The explanation is detailed enough for a reader unfamiliar with the topic to grasp context, yet a few contextual points (e.g. wider industry privacy concerns) are only briefly noted, so the response falls just short of the very top of the band.

Required EE sections (title page, contents, introduction, methodology, analysis, conclusion, bibliography, appendix) are all present and logically ordered.  Some headings are uneven (duplicate Table of Contents entries), but overall structure is fully appropriate, qualifying for the upper band.

annotations.comment: The inclusion of a clear title page with the investigation title, word count, session and candidate code demonstrates adherence to formatting requirements, ensuring administrative elements are correctly presented.
The claim that the United Nations âestablished a lawâ on privacy is factually inaccurate. Revise to reference specific dataâprotection regulations (e.g. GDPR) accurately.
This paragraph reiterates points from the SWOT section without new analysis. Consider editing for conciseness or integrating fresh insights to maintain focus.
The âDiscussion:â heading appears without introductory analysis below it. Ensure that section headings match content and flow logically.
The PESTLE analysis lists UN legislative action incorrectly under political factors. Verify the source and correct to specific regulatory bodies or legislation.
The research question spans two lines and is somewhat wordy. Consider tightening phrasing (e.g. removing filler words) to improve clarity and focus from the outset.
The table of contents is comprehensive but shows duplicate or uneven heading entries. Review and consolidate entries to ensure structure is clearly signposted.

The methodology section acknowledges only secondary sources and their limitations without demonstrating triangulation or primary data collection. Consider incorporating or recommending primary research to strengthen validity.
The introductory paragraph relies on a secondary citation chain (âas cited by Rehman, 2019â) without the original source. Provide direct citations or verify primary sources to ensure accuracy.
The SWOT tool is described coherently with clear definition of strengths and weaknesses and acknowledges limitations. This demonstrates solid understanding of the analytical concept.
The PESTLE tool description repeats limitations noted in SWOT without adding new insights on selection rationale. Try linking each tool more explicitly to the research question.
The SWOT analysis table integrates internal and external factors well, but many points lack supporting evidence or data references. Cite specific data for each bullet to strengthen credibility.
The caption âTable 1 Swot Matrixâ lacks consistent styling (capitalization, positioning). Standardize caption formatting and placement directly under the figure.
The valuation ratio trends are interpreted effectively, showing a strong grasp of how P/E and P/B movements reflect investor sentiment.
The phrase âscandalapproximatelyâ lacks a space.
The conclusion restates findings but offers limited new recommendations. Deepen critical evaluation by ranking strategies or suggesting further research directions.
Bibliography entries show inconsistent formatting styles and indentation. Ensure all references follow a single citation format (e.g., APA) and align hanging indents.
The appendix equation for net profit margin is cut off and misaligned. Format equations fully and consistently for readability.
Several cells in the economic column are left blank, weakening completeness of the external analysis. Populate each PESTLE dimension for balance.
The âAnalysis and discussionâ label is placed before the PESTLE table. Consider moving this header after the table to improve structure.
A specific citation is needed for the claim of a 19% stock price drop and user losses. Include the data source or reference for accuracy.
The description of AIâpowered ad targeting effectively links technological response to the scandalâs impact, showing clear analysis of external factors.
The stakeholder map clearly identifies groups by power and interest, demonstrating effective application of business mapping tools.
The image caption is formatted as body text rather than a formal caption below the figure. Revise styling (font, alignment) to match EE conventions.
The financial analysis introduction outlines ratio categories and data sources clearly, providing a strong methodological foundation for the quantitative section.
Figure 1 lacks axis labels, units or data source notation. Add clear labels and references so readers can verify the plotted data.
Causal links between ratio changes and scandal effects are asserted without benchmarking or comparative data. Consider adding context or statistical testing to support these conclusions.
The efficiency ratio definition is concise and clearly linked to its application, demonstrating accurate use of financial terminology.
The financial calculations in the appendix are clear and correctly formatted, providing transparency in how ratios were derived.
